name: Listmonk
what: Self-hosted newsletter and mailing-list manager with campaigns, subscribers, templates, and APIs.
solves: Email newsletters, list management, segmentation, subscription handling, and lightweight marketing messaging.
users: Marketing and content teams that want a simple, self-hosted newsletter system.
fit: Good fit for straightforward newsletter and broadcast email. Cleaner and lighter than Mautic when advanced marketing automation is not yet necessary.
runtime: Single binary/web app with PostgreSQL and SMTP/provider integration.
components: Admin UI, campaign engine, subscriber/list management, templates, API, and optional media storage.
deploy: Relatively easy self-host path; PostgreSQL and SMTP/provider config are the main requirements.
deps: PostgreSQL, SMTP or email provider, reverse proxy/TLS, and media/file storage if using uploads.
auth: Local admin users plus API tokens; OIDC support exists.
apis: REST API, import/export, webhooks/integrations, and campaign/list operations.
security: Subscriber data and marketing consent must be handled carefully; keep admin access tight and double-check unsubscribe/consent flows.
setup_path: Use official docs and single-binary/container deployment. Keep it narrow: newsletters and simple campaigns.
env: DB URL, app/admin config, SMTP/provider credentials, media storage, and base URL.
persistence: Back up PostgreSQL and any media storage; preserve template/campaign history where needed.
operations: Deliverability, unsubscribe correctness, bounce handling, and consent-data integrity matter most.
failure_points: Treating it like a full customer-data platform or duplicating preference management poorly with Novu/Mautic/site DB.
integration: Strong fit for newsletter signups, product updates, and content broadcasts from the Hylono site. Can integrate with Next.js, n8n, and PostHog audiences/exports.
verify: Decide whether newsletter preferences live in Listmonk, the site DB, or both with synchronization rules.
gdpr: Explicit consent, unsubscribe, and retention/delete flows matter.
overlap: Preferred over Mautic for simple newsletters; separate from Novu’s transactional role.
codex_read_first: Read newsletter-signup flows and current Resend usage before implementing.
codex_best_path: Use Listmonk for newsletters/broadcasts only; keep transactional messaging in Novu/Resend.
codex_never_assume: Never merge marketing and transactional preference logic without an explicit model.
